title: Issue 2233 session log local boundary

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Remove obsolete promotion/share-safety framing from ordinary session-log surfaces and replace it with a local-only non-authoritative diagnostic boundary.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not build a promotion/privacy subsystem, redact or rewrite raw local capture by default, or treat manual human handoff as AW-owned.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact local-boundary metadata shape, generated command wording, and focused regression coverage for status, prelude, index, export, and analysis.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires changing the local override config contract, removing path normalization compatibility, or introducing a managed transfer workflow.
canonical_core.next_action: Commit the validated change and open the #2233 PR.
proof_expectations: Focused lint passes for changed Python tests/source.
completion_criteria: Status, prelude, index, export, and analysis expose local-only/non-authoritative boundary language.
Ordinary session-log output no longer claims promotion, share-safety, disclosure review, or transfer ownership.
Raw local capture remains unchanged by default; export creates a separate local bundle with known local paths normalized.
Regression coverage checks the clarified metadata across supported logging surfaces.

goal: Remove obsolete promotion/share-safety framing from ordinary session-log surfaces and replace it with a local-only non-authoritative diagnostic boundary.
non_goals: No promotion framework, privacy subsystem, managed transfer workflow, raw-capture rewrite, or removal of path normalization compatibility.
